title: Q1 2026 Compliance Review Summary
body:
COMPLIANCE REVIEW SUMMARY — Q1 2026

Report Date: March 1, 2026
Review Period: January 1 – March 1, 2026
Prepared By: Legal & Compliance Officer

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

TechCorp's Q1 2026 compliance review confirms that all critical compliance areas remain in good standing with no outstanding critical items. Our mature SaaS platform continues to operate under established data governance practices, access controls, and incident response protocols. This quarter included the completion of our scheduled incident response drill, validation of data handling practices, and successful access control audit. All recent policy updates have been implemented in accordance with regulatory guidance and internal standards.

COMPLIANCE AREA REVIEWS

1. Data Handling Practices — COMPLIANT
Our data handling practices remain compliant with applicable data protection regulations and company policy. All employee data processed through PeopleSync Data Processor Agreement (Document ID: comp_dpa_peoplesync_2026_001) is handled in accordance with agreed data categories (name, email, role, department) without embedding personally identifiable information in system records. Documentation demonstrates clear separation of PII handling from operational systems, meeting both legal obligations and internal security standards.

2. Access Control Audit — PASSED
The Q1 access control audit has been completed and passed with no deficiencies. Internal security policy updates reflecting the 90-day to 60-day password rotation requirement change (per security audit recommendation) have been implemented in Policy ID: INT-SEC-2026-001. All access reviews confirm proper segregation of duties, timely provisioning/deprovisioning, and alignment with least-privilege principles. Documentation is maintained and audit-ready.

3. Incident Response Drill — COMPLETED
The Q1 incident response drill was successfully completed on February 15, 2026. The drill validated our incident detection, escalation, and communication proc
